1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# menu.sh - Interactive Workspace selection menu
     3→
     4→# Module-level arrays (no nameref needed, bash 3.2 compatible)
     5→_CW_MENU_PATHS=()
     6→_CW_MENU_NAMES=()
     7→_CW_MENU_TIMES=()
     8→_CW_MENU_REGISTERED=()
     9→
    10→# ──────────────────────────────
    11→# Build workspace list into module arrays
    12→# ──────────────────────────────
    13→_cw_menu_build_list() {
    14→  _CW_MENU_PATHS=()
    15→  _CW_MENU_NAMES=()
    16→  _CW_MENU_TIMES=()
    17→  _CW_MENU_REGISTERED=()
    18→
    19→  local ws_list
    20→  ws_list=$(registry_list)
    21→
    22→  while IFS= read -r ws; do
    23→    local name path last_used
    24→    name=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.name')
    25→    path=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.path')
    26→    last_used=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.last_used // ""')
    27→    _CW_MENU_PATHS+=("$path")
    28→    _CW_MENU_NAMES+=("$name")
    29→    _CW_MENU_TIMES+=("$last_used")
    30→    _CW_MENU_REGISTERED+=("true")
    31→  done < <(echo "$ws_list" | jq -c '.[]' 2>/dev/null)
    32→
    33→  local search_base="${WORKING_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/WorkingProjects}"
    34→  if [[ -d "$search_base" ]]; then
    35→    while IFS= read -r ws_file; do
    36→      local ws_dir ws_name
    37→      ws_dir=$(dirname "$ws_file")
    38→      ws_name=$(ws_get "$ws_file" '.name')
    39→      local already=false
    40→      local p
    41→      for p in "${_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]+"${_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]}"}"; do
    42→        [[ "$p" == "$ws_dir" ]] && already=true && break
    43→      done
    44→      $already && continue
    45→      _CW_MENU_PATHS+=("$ws_dir")
    46→      _CW_MENU_NAMES+=("$ws_name")
    47→      _CW_MENU_TIMES+=("")
    48→      _CW_MENU_REGISTERED+=("false")
    49→    done < <(find "$search_base" -maxdepth 2 -name ".workspace.json" 2>/dev/null | sort)
    50→  fi
    51→}
    52→
    53→# ──────────────────────────────
    54→# Main entry point
    55→# ──────────────────────────────
    56→cmd_menu() {
    57→  require_jq
    58→  _cw_menu_build_list
    59→
    60→  while true; do
    61→    local selected_path
    62→    if command -v fzf &>/dev/null; then
    63→      selected_path=$(_menu_fzf_pick)
    64→    else
    65→      selected_path=$(_menu_numbered_pick)
    66→    fi
    67→
    68→    # Empty = Esc/quit
    69→    if [[ -z "$selected_path" ]]; then
    70→      echo ""
    71→      info "$(t "cmd_quit")"
    72→      return 0
    73→    fi
    74→
    75→    # Create new workspace
    76→    if [[ "$selected_path" == "CREATE_NEW" ]]; then
    77→      cmd_new
    78→      return
    79→    fi
    80→
    81→    # Cleanup registry
    82→    if [[ "$selected_path" == "CLEANUP" ]]; then
    83→      _menu_cleanup
    84→      _cw_menu_build_list
    85→      continue
    86→    fi
    87→
    88→    # Auto-register if unregistered
    89→    local idx
    90→    for idx in "${!_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]}"; do
    91→      if [[ "${_CW_MENU_PATHS[$idx]}" == "$selected_path" ]]; then
    92→        if [[ "${_CW_MENU_REGISTERED[$idx]}" == "false" ]]; then
    93→          registry_add "${_CW_MENU_NAMES[$idx]}" "$selected_path"
    94→          success "$(t "registered"): ${_CW_MENU_NAMES[$idx]}"
    95→        fi
    96→        break
    97→      fi
    98→    done
    99→
   100→    if [[ ! -d "$selected_path" ]]; then
   101→      error "$(t "dir_not_found"): $selected_path"
   102→      continue
   103→    fi
   104→
   105→    local ws_name
   106→    ws_name=$(jq -r '.name' "${selected_path}/${WORKSPACE_FILE}" 2>/dev/null)
   107→
   108→    # Show submenu and handle result
   109→    local submenu_result
   110→    submenu_result=$(_menu_submenu_pick "$selected_path" "$ws_name")
   111→
   112→    case "$submenu_result" in
   113→      LAUNCH)
   114→        cd "$selected_path"
   115→        cmd_launch
   116→        return
   117→        ;;
   118→      ADD_DIR)
   119→        (cd "$selected_path" && cmd_add_dir) || true
   120→        _cw_menu_build_list
   121→        # Re-show submenu after add-dir
   122→        submenu_result=$(_menu_submenu_pick "$selected_path" "$ws_name")
   123→        [[ "$submenu_result" == "LAUNCH" ]] && { cd "$selected_path"; cmd_launch; return; }
   124→        continue
   125→        ;;
   126→      INFO)
   127→        (cd "$selected_path" && cmd_info) || true
   128→        echo ""
   129→        read -rp "  $(t "press_enter"): " _
   130→        continue
   131→        ;;
   132→      FINDER)
   133→        open "$selected_path"
   134→        continue
   135→        ;;
   136→      FORGET)
   137→        _menu_forget "$selected_path" "$ws_name"
   138→        _cw_menu_build_list
   139→        continue
   140→        ;;
   141→      *)
   142→        # BACK or empty — loop back to main list
   143→        continue
   144→        ;;
   145→    esac
   146→  done
   147→}
   148→
   149→# ──────────────────────────────
   150→# fzf picker: returns selected PATH or CREATE_NEW or empty string
   151→# ──────────────────────────────
   152→_menu_fzf_pick() {
   153→  # Build tab-separated fzf input: PATH\tDISPLAY
   154→  local fzf_entries=""
   155→  fzf_entries+=$'CREATE_NEW\t'"$(t "menu_create_new")"$'\n'
   156→  fzf_entries+=$'CLEANUP\t'"$(t "menu_cleanup")"$'\n'
   157→
   158→  local idx
   159→  for idx in "${!_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]}"; do
   160→    local path="${_CW_MENU_PATHS[$idx]}"
   161→    local name="${_CW_MENU_NAMES[$idx]}"
   162→    local last_used="${_CW_MENU_TIMES[$idx]}"
   163→    local reg="${_CW_MENU_REGISTERED[$idx]}"
   164→
   165→    local time_label=""
   166→    if [[ -n "$last_used" ]]; then
   167→      local rel
   168→      rel=$(_relative_time "$last_used")
   169→      [[ -n "$rel" ]] && time_label="  $(dim "$rel")"
   170→    fi
   171→
   172→    local extra_label=""
   173→    [[ "$reg" == "false" ]] && extra_label+="  $(yellow "[unregistered]")"
   174→    [[ ! -d "$path" ]] && extra_label+="  $(red "[missing]")"
   175→
   176→    fzf_entries+="${path}"$'\t'"${name}${time_label}${extra_label}"$'\n'
   177→  done
   178→
   179→  # Preview script (no bash vars — uses fzf {1} placeholder)
   180→  local preview_script
   181→  preview_script=$(cat <<'PREVIEW'
   182→ws_path={1}
   183→ws_file="${ws_path}/.workspace.json"
   184→if [[ "$ws_path" == "CREATE_NEW" ]]; then
   185→  echo ""
   186→  echo "  Create a new Claude Workspace"
   187→  echo ""
   188→  echo "  A new directory will be created under"
   189→  echo "  ~/WorkingProjects/ and set up for cw."
   190→elif [[ "$ws_path" == "CLEANUP" ]]; then
   191→  echo ""
   192→  echo "  Cleanup registry"
   193→  echo ""
   194→  echo "  Removes entries whose workspace directory"
   195→  echo "  no longer exists on disk."
   196→elif [[ -f "$ws_file" ]]; then
   197→  name=$(jq -r '.name // ""' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
   198→  desc=$(jq -r '.description // ""' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
   199→  created=$(jq -r '.created_at // ""' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
   200→  dir_count=$(jq '.dirs | length' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   201→  echo ""
   202→  echo "  $name"
   203→  [[ -n "$desc" ]] && echo "  $desc"
   204→  echo ""
   205→  echo "  $ws_path"
   206→  echo "  Dirs: $dir_count    Created: ${created:0:10}"
   207→  echo ""
   208→  while IFS= read -r dir_entry; do
   209→    dir_path=$(echo "$dir_entry" | jq -r '.path' 2>/dev/null)
   210→    dir_role=$(echo "$dir_entry" | jq -r '.role // ""' 2>/dev/null)
   211→    label="${dir_role:-$(basename "$dir_path")}"
   212→    if [[ -d "$dir_path" ]]; then
   213→      echo "  ✓  $label"
   214→    else
   215→      echo "  ✗  $label  (missing)"
   216→    fi
   217→  done < <(jq -c '.dirs[]' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
   218→else
   219→  echo ""
   220→  echo "  (no .workspace.json found)"
   221→  echo "  $ws_path"
   222→fi
   223→PREVIEW
   224→)
   225→
   226→  local selected
   227→  selected=$(printf '%s' "$fzf_entries" | \
   228→    fzf --ansi \
   229→        --height=70% --border \
   230→        --delimiter=$'\t' \
   231→        --with-nth='2..' \
   232→        --header="  claude-workspace (cw)" \
   233→        --header-first \
   234→        --preview="$preview_script" \
   235→        --preview-window='right:45%:wrap' \
   236→        --prompt='  Workspace > ' \
   237→        2>/dev/null || true)
   238→
   239→  [[ -z "$selected" ]] && echo "" && return 0
   240→
   241→  # Return the PATH (field 1, before the tab)
   242→  printf '%s' "$selected" | cut -f1
   243→}
   244→
   245→# ──────────────────────────────
   246→# Numbered picker (no fzf): returns selected PATH or CREATE_NEW or empty
   247→# ──────────────────────────────
   248→_menu_numbered_pick() {
   249→  echo "" >&2
   250→  echo "$(bold "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")" >&2
   251→  echo "$(bold "  claude-workspace (cw)")" >&2
   252→  echo "$(bold "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")" >&2
   253→
   254→  if [[ ${#_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
   255→    echo "" >&2
   256→    info "$(t "workspace_not_found")" >&2
   257→    echo "" >&2
   258→    read -rep "  $(t "new_workspace")? [Y/n]: " ans
   259→    if [[ ! "$ans" =~ ^[Nn]$ ]]; then
   260→      echo "CREATE_NEW"
   261→    else
   262→      echo ""
   263→    fi
   264→    return 0
   265→  fi
   266→
   267→  echo "" >&2
   268→  echo "  $(bold "$(t "menu_recent")")" >&2
   269→  echo "" >&2
   270→
   271→  local i=0
   272→  local idx
   273→  for idx in "${!_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]}"; do
   274→    local path="${_CW_MENU_PATHS[$idx]}"
   275→    local name="${_CW_MENU_NAMES[$idx]}"
   276→    local last_used="${_CW_MENU_TIMES[$idx]}"
   277→    local registered="${_CW_MENU_REGISTERED[$idx]}"
   278→    i=$((i + 1))
   279→
   280→    local status_marker=""
   281→    if [[ ! -d "$path" ]]; then
   282→      status_marker="  $(red "✗ $(t "path_missing")")"
   283→    elif [[ "$path" == "$(pwd)" ]]; then
   284→      status_marker="  $(green "← $(t "current_location")")"
   285→    fi
   286→
   287→    local unreg_label=""
   288→    [[ "$registered" == "false" ]] && unreg_label="  $(yellow "$(t "unregistered")")"
   289→
   290→    local time_label=""
   291→    if [[ -n "$last_used" ]]; then
   292→      local rel
   293→      rel=$(_relative_time "$last_used")
   294→      [[ -n "$rel" ]] && time_label="  $(dim "$rel")"
   295→    fi
   296→
   297→    printf "  [%d] $(bold "%s")%s%s%s\n" "$i" "$name" "$time_label" "$unreg_label" "$status_marker" >&2
   298→    printf "      $(dim "%s")\n" "$path" >&2
   299→    echo "" >&2
   300→  done
   301→
   302→  echo "  $(dim "────────────────────────────────────────")" >&2
   303→  echo "  [N] $(t "menu_create_new")" >&2
   304→  echo "  [C] $(t "menu_cleanup")" >&2
   305→  echo "  [Q] $(t "menu_quit")" >&2
   306→  echo "" >&2
   307→
   308→  local choice
   309→  read -rep "  $(t "cmd_select") [1-${i} / N / C / Q]: " choice
   310→  echo "" >&2
   311→
   312→  case "$choice" in
   313→    [Nn])
   314→      echo "CREATE_NEW"
   315→      ;;
   316→    [Cc])
   317→      echo "CLEANUP"
   318→      ;;
   319→    [Qq]|"")
   320→      echo ""
   321→      ;;
   322→    *)
   323→      if [[ "$choice" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && \
   324→         [[ $choice -ge 1 ]] && \
   325→         [[ $choice -le ${#_CW_MENU_PATHS[@]} ]]; then
   326→        local sel_idx=$(( choice - 1 ))
   327→        echo "${_CW_MENU_PATHS[$sel_idx]}"
   328→      else
   329→        error "$(t "invalid"): $choice" >&2
   330→        echo ""
   331→      fi
   332→      ;;
   333→  esac
   334→}
   335→
   336→# ──────────────────────────────
   337→# Submenu picker: returns action code
   338→# Returns: LAUNCH, ADD_DIR, INFO, FINDER, FORGET, BACK, or empty
   339→# ──────────────────────────────
   340→_menu_submenu_pick() {
   341→  local ws_path="$1"
   342→  local ws_name="$2"
   343→
   344→  local resume_label add_dir_label info_label finder_label forget_label back_label
   345→  resume_label=$(t "menu_action_resume")
   346→  add_dir_label=$(t "menu_action_add_dir")
   347→  info_label=$(t "menu_action_info")
   348→  finder_label=$(t "menu_action_finder")
   349→  forget_label=$(t "menu_action_forget")
   350→  back_label=$(t "menu_action_back")
   351→
   352→  local action
   353→  if command -v fzf &>/dev/null; then
   354→    action=$(printf '%s\n' \
   355→      "$resume_label" \
   356→      "$add_dir_label" \
   357→      "$info_label" \
   358→      "$finder_label" \
   359→      "$forget_label" \
   360→      "$back_label" | \
   361→      fzf --height=40% --border \
   362→          --header="  $ws_name" \
   363→          --header-first \
   364→          --prompt='  Action > ' \
   365→          2>/dev/null || true)
   366→  else
   367→    # Numbered submenu fallback (no fzf)
   368→    echo "" >&2
   369→    echo "  $(bold "$ws_name")" >&2
   370→    echo "" >&2
   371→    echo "  1) $resume_label" >&2
   372→    echo "  2) $add_dir_label" >&2
   373→    echo "  3) $info_label" >&2
   374→    echo "  4) $finder_label" >&2
   375→    echo "  5) $forget_label" >&2
   376→    echo "  6) $back_label" >&2
   377→    echo "" >&2
   378→    local sub_choice
   379→    read -rep "  Select [1-6]: " sub_choice
   380→    case "$sub_choice" in
   381→      1) action="$resume_label" ;;
   382→      2) action="$add_dir_label" ;;
   383→      3) action="$info_label" ;;
   384→      4) action="$finder_label" ;;
   385→      5) action="$forget_label" ;;
   386→      6) action="$back_label" ;;
   387→      *) action="$back_label" ;;
   388→    esac
   389→  fi
   390→
   391→  if [[ -z "$action" ]] || [[ "$action" == "$back_label" ]]; then
   392→    echo "BACK"
   393→  elif [[ "$action" == "$resume_label" ]]; then
   394→    echo "LAUNCH"
   395→  elif [[ "$action" == "$add_dir_label" ]]; then
   396→    echo "ADD_DIR"
   397→  elif [[ "$action" == "$info_label" ]]; then
   398→    echo "INFO"
   399→  elif [[ "$action" == "$finder_label" ]]; then
   400→    echo "FINDER"
   401→  elif [[ "$action" == "$forget_label" ]]; then
   402→    echo "FORGET"
   403→  else
   404→    echo "BACK"
   405→  fi
   406→}
   407→
   408→# ──────────────────────────────
   409→# Forget workspace (no pwd dependency)
   410→# ──────────────────────────────
   411→_menu_forget() {
   412→  local ws_path="$1"
   413→  local ws_name="$2"
   414→
   415→  # Step 1: Pick mode via fzf (or numbered fallback)
   416→  local registry_only_label delete_dir_label
   417→  registry_only_label=$(t "forget_mode_registry")
   418→  delete_dir_label=$(t "forget_mode_delete")
   419→
   420→  local mode
   421→  if command -v fzf &>/dev/null; then
   422→    mode=$(printf '%s\n' "$registry_only_label" "$delete_dir_label" | \
   423→      fzf --height=30% --border \
   424→          --header="  $ws_name" \
   425→          --header-first \
   426→          --prompt='  Delete mode > ' \
   427→          2>/dev/null || true)
   428→  else
   429→    # Numbered fallback (output to stderr to avoid stdout pollution)
   430→    echo "" >&2
   431→    echo "  1) $registry_only_label" >&2
   432→    echo "  2) $delete_dir_label" >&2
   433→    echo "" >&2
   434→    local choice
   435→    read -rp "  Select [1/2]: " choice
   436→    case "$choice" in
   437→      1) mode="$registry_only_label" ;;
   438→      2) mode="$delete_dir_label" ;;
   439→      *) mode="" ;;
   440→    esac
   441→  fi
   442→
   443→  [[ -z "$mode" ]] && return  # Esc -> back to sub-menu
   444→
   445→  if [[ "$mode" == "$registry_only_label" ]]; then
   446→    # Registry only (current behavior)
   447→    echo ""
   448→    warn "\"${ws_name}\" $(t "remove_from_registry")"
   449→    echo "  $(dim "$(t "files_remain")")"
   450→    echo ""
   451→    read -rp "  $(t "continue") [y/N]: " confirm
   452→    if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
   453→      registry_remove "$ws_path"
   454→      success "$(t "deleted"): $ws_name"
   455→      echo ""
   456→    else
   457→      info "$(t "cancel")"
   458→    fi
   459→
   460→  elif [[ "$mode" == "$delete_dir_label" ]]; then
   461→    # Safety check: must be under WorkingProjects/
   462→    local wp_base="${WORKING_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/WorkingProjects}"
   463→    wp_base=$(expand_path "$wp_base")
   464→    if [[ "$ws_path" != "$wp_base"/* ]]; then
   465→      echo ""
   466→      error "$(t "forget_delete_outside_wp")"
   467→      echo "  $(dim "$ws_path")"
   468→      echo ""
   469→      return  # back to sub-menu
   470→    fi
   471→
   472→    # Destructive confirmation
   473→    echo ""
   474→    warn "$(t "forget_delete_confirm"): \"$ws_name\""
   475→    echo "  $(dim "$ws_path")"
   476→    warn "$(t "irreversible")"
   477→    echo ""
   478→    read -rp "  $(t "continue") [y/N]: " confirm
   479→    if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
   480→      registry_remove "$ws_path"
   481→      rm -rf "$ws_path"
   482→      success "$(t "forget_dir_deleted"): $ws_name"
   483→      echo ""
   484→    else
   485→      info "$(t "cancel")"
   486→    fi
   487→  fi
   488→}
   489→
   490→# ──────────────────────────────
   491→# Cleanup registry: remove entries for missing directories
   492→# ──────────────────────────────
   493→_menu_cleanup() {
   494→  local missing_paths=()
   495→  local missing_names=()
   496→
   497→  local ws_list
   498→  ws_list=$(registry_list)
   499→
   500→  while IFS= read -r ws; do
   501→    local name path
   502→    name=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.name')
   503→    path=$(echo "$ws" | jq -r '.path')
   504→    if [[ ! -d "$path" ]]; then
   505→      missing_paths+=("$path")
   506→      missing_names+=("$name")
   507→    fi
   508→  done < <(echo "$ws_list" | jq -c '.[]' 2>/dev/null)
   509→
   510→  if [[ ${#missing_paths[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
   511→    echo ""
   512→    info "$(t "cleanup_none")"
   513→    echo ""
   514→    read -rp "  $(t "press_enter")" _
   515→    return
   516→  fi
   517→
   518→  echo ""
   519→  warn "${#missing_paths[@]} $(t "cleanup_found")"
   520→  echo ""
   521→  local i
   522→  for i in "${!missing_paths[@]}"; do
   523→    echo "  $(red "✗")  ${missing_names[$i]}  $(dim "${missing_paths[$i]}")"
   524→  done
   525→  echo ""
   526→  read -rp "  $(t "continue") [y/N]: " confirm
   527→  if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
   528→    local path
   529→    for path in "${missing_paths[@]+"${missing_paths[@]}"}"; do
   530→      registry_remove "$path"
   531→    done
   532→    success "${#missing_paths[@]} $(t "cleanup_done")"
   533→    echo ""
   534→  else
   535→    info "$(t "cancel")"
   536→  fi
   537→}

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# utils.sh - Common utilities
     3→
     4→# ──────────────────────────────
     5→# Colors / Styles
     6→# ──────────────────────────────
     7→bold()    { echo -e "\033[1m$*\033[0m"; }
     8→green()   { echo -e "\033[32m$*\033[0m"; }
     9→yellow()  { echo -e "\033[33m$*\033[0m"; }
    10→cyan()    { echo -e "\033[36m$*\033[0m"; }
    11→red()     { echo -e "\033[31m$*\033[0m"; }
    12→dim()     { echo -e "\033[2m$*\033[0m"; }
    13→
    14→info()    { echo "  $(cyan "ℹ") $*"; }
    15→success() { echo "  $(green "✓") $*"; }
    16→warn()    { echo "  $(yellow "⚠") $*" >&2; }
    17→error()   { echo "  $(red "✗") $*" >&2; }
    18→step()    { echo ""; echo "$(bold "→ $*")"; }
    19→
    20→# ──────────────────────────────
    21→# Workspace file paths
    22→# ──────────────────────────────
    23→WORKSPACE_FILE=".workspace.json"
    24→WORKSPACE_CLAUDE_MD="CLAUDE.md"
    25→
    26→workspace_file() {
    27→  echo "${1:-.}/$WORKSPACE_FILE"
    28→}
    29→
    30→is_workspace() {
    31→  local dir="${1:-.}"
    32→  [[ -f "$dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE" ]]
    33→}
    34→
    35→# ──────────────────────────────
    36→# JSON operations (jq required)
    37→# ──────────────────────────────
    38→require_jq() {
    39→  if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
    40→    error "$(t "error_jq_required")"
    41→    exit 1
    42→  fi
    43→}
    44→
    45→# Read workspace.json
    46→ws_get() {
    47→  local file="$1" key="$2"
    48→  jq -r "$key" "$file" 2>/dev/null
    49→}
    50→
    51→# Update workspace.json
    52→# Usage: ws_set <file> [--arg key val ...] <filter>
    53→ws_set() {
    54→  local file="$1"
    55→  shift
    56→  local tmp
    57→  tmp=$(mktemp)
    58→  jq "$@" "$file" > "$tmp" && mv "$tmp" "$file"
    59→}
    60→
    61→# ──────────────────────────────
    62→# Path normalization
    63→# ──────────────────────────────
    64→normalize_path() {
    65→  local path="$1"
    66→  # Expand ~
    67→  path="${path/#\~/$HOME}"
    68→  # Remove trailing slash
    69→  path="${path%/}"
    70→  echo "$path"
    71→}
    72→
    73→expand_path() {
    74→  local path
    75→  path=$(normalize_path "$1")
    76→  # Use realpath if available (otherwise return as is)
    77→  if command -v realpath &>/dev/null; then
    78→    realpath -m "$path" 2>/dev/null || echo "$path"
    79→  else
    80→    echo "$path"
    81→  fi
    82→}
    83→
    84→# Pick a directory with fzf (if available) or fall back to read.
    85→# Arguments: <prompt_label>
    86→# Outputs:   absolute path echoed to stdout, or empty string if cancelled
    87→select_dir_with_fzf() {
    88→  local prompt="${1:-}"
    89→  local result=""
    90→
    91→  if command -v fzf &>/dev/null; then
    92→    result=$(
    93→      find "$HOME" -maxdepth 4 -type d \
    94→        \( -path "$HOME/Library" -o -name ".git" -o -name "node_modules" \
    95→           -o -name ".cache" -o -name "__pycache__" \) -prune \
    96→        -o -type d -print 2>/dev/null \
    97→      | fzf --height=40% --border \
    98→            --prompt="$prompt > " \
    99→            --preview="ls {}" \
   100→            --preview-window=right:40% \
   101→      || true
   102→    )
   103→    [[ -n "$result" ]] && result=$(expand_path "$result")
   104→  else
   105→    read -rep "  $prompt: " result
   106→    [[ -n "$result" ]] && result=$(expand_path "$result")
   107→  fi
   108→
   109→  echo "$result"
   110→}
   111→
   112→# ──────────────────────────────
   113→# Relative time display (e.g., "3 days ago")
   114→# Arguments: <ISO8601 UTC timestamp>
   115→# ──────────────────────────────
   116→_relative_time() {
   117→  local ts="$1"
   118→  [[ -z "$ts" ]] && echo "" && return
   119→
   120→  # Remove milliseconds (2025-06-01T12:00:00.123Z -> 2025-06-01T12:00:00Z)
   121→  ts="${ts%%.*}Z"
   122→
   123→  local now
   124→  now=$(date -u +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   125→
   126→  local then=0
   127→  # Support both macOS (BSD date) and Linux (GNU date)
   128→  # Use -u flag to interpret as UTC (handles Z suffix correctly)
   129→  if then=$(date -u -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" "$ts" +%s 2>/dev/null); then
   130→    : # macOS success
   131→  elif then=$(date -u -d "${ts}" +%s 2>/dev/null); then
   132→    : # Linux success
   133→  else
   134→    echo "" && return
   135→  fi
   136→
   137→  local diff=$(( now - then ))
   138→  if   [[ $diff -lt 60 ]];      then t "just_now"
   139→  elif [[ $diff -lt 3600 ]];    then echo "$(( diff / 60 ))$(t "minutes_ago")"
   140→  elif [[ $diff -lt 86400 ]];   then echo "$(( diff / 3600 ))$(t "hours_ago")"
   141→  elif [[ $diff -lt 604800 ]];  then echo "$(( diff / 86400 ))$(t "days_ago")"
   142→  elif [[ $diff -lt 2592000 ]]; then echo "$(( diff / 604800 ))$(t "weeks_ago")"
   143→  else echo "$(( diff / 2592000 ))$(t "months_ago")"
   144→  fi
   145→}
   146→

コードベースを把握しました。仕様書のレビューを行います。

---

## APPROVE（条件付き）

判定理由: 仕様書は全体的に実装開始できる水準に達しています。既存コードとの整合性も概ね取れており、アーキテクチャの方針も明確です。ただし、実装者が迷う可能性のある曖昧な箇所が3点あり、着手前に明確化することを推奨します。

---

## 各基準スコア

| 基準 | スコア | 評価 |
|------|--------|------|
| Clarity（明確さ） | 3 / 5 | `gum+fzf` のTab連携の実装方法、Phase 3で Launch を選んだ後の `cmd_new` のフローが曖昧 |
| Verifiability（検証可能性） | 4 / 5 | Success Criteria は具体的でテスト可能。ただし「全 [y/N] プロンプトを置き換え」は対象ファイルの網羅性が未確認 |
| Completeness（完全性） | 4 / 5 | 必要な情報はほぼ揃っている。`gum_choose` のフォールバック実装例が一部矛盾あり（後述） |
| Consistency（一貫性） | 3 / 5 | `gum_choose` のフォールバック説明が2箇所で食い違っている、`menu.sh` の変更内容が不明確 |

---

## 問題点と改善案（上位3件）

### 1. `gum_choose` フォールバックの説明が矛盾している

**該当箇所（2箇所）:**

Architecture セクション:
```
gum_choose <options...> → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
                           (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
```

Fallback Behavior セクション:
```
gum_choose → numbered menu (inline numbered list via echo + read)
```

これ自体は一致しているように見えますが、Phase 3 の `gum choose` に渡す3つの選択肢（`phase3_opt_launch`, `phase3_opt_create`, `phase3_opt_cancel`）の **返り値の扱い** が仕様書に書かれていません。`gum choose` は選択された文字列をそのまま stdout に返すため、`cmd_setup` の Phase 3 関数内でその文字列を何と比較して分岐するのか（i18n キーの値と比較? 内部定数と比較?）が不明確です。

**改善案:** Phase 3 の選択肢の比較ロジックを明示すること。例:
```
gum_choose の戻り値を case 文で比較する。
比較対象は t "phase3_opt_launch" の展開値とする。
```

---

### 2. `gum+fzf` のTab連携の実装方法が未定義

**該当箇所:**
```
gum + fzf both available: gum input for typing; pressing Tab launches fzf to browse
```

`gum input` はターミナルの生のキー入力を受け取る UI コンポーネントであり、Tab キーのフックを外部スクリプトから制御する標準的な手段がありません。実際には `gum input` の `--timeout` や終了コードで Tab を検知する方法は存在せず、実装不可能な記述になっている可能性があります。

**改善案:** この挙動の実現方法を明示するか、実装上の制約として認め、`gum+fzf` の場合も fzf をデフォルトのパスブラウザとして起動する（gum input は表示テキストとして使用しない）などの代替仕様に修正すること。

---

### 3. `cw new` の Phase 3「Create & Launch」後のフローに潜在的な二重起動リスク

**該当箇所:**
```
cw new calls cmd_setup and then returns; Phase 3 inside cmd_setup handles the launch 
decision, so cmd_new does not add a separate cmd_launch call
```

現在の `lib/new.sh` の `cmd_new` には `cmd_setup "$ws_name"` の後に明示的な `cmd_launch` 呼び出しがありません（確認済み）。この点は仕様書の記述と一致しています。

ただし、`cmd_new` の中で `cd "$target_dir"` を実行した後に `cmd_setup` を呼ぶ実装になっており（現コード line 75-76）、Phase 3 の中で `cmd_launch` を呼ぶと、`cmd_launch` は `pwd` ベースで動作するため、`target_dir` が正しい `pwd` であることが前提になります。Phase 3 が実行されるのは `cmd_setup` 内部（= `cd` 後）であるため実際は問題ないはずですが、仕様書には **`cmd_setup` が `pwd` に依存することを前提とした呼び出しである**という制約が明記されていません。

**改善案:** `cmd_setup` の Phase 3 で `cmd_launch` を呼ぶ際、`pwd` ではなく `target_dir` 変数（`_setup_phase1/2/3` に受け渡された値）を明示的に使用することを仕様に記載すること。または `cmd_launch` のシグネチャを `cmd_launch [dir]` に拡張する方針を明記すること。

---

## その他の軽微な指摘（判定には影響しない）

- `menu.sh` が「Modify — replace confirm patterns」とだけ書かれているが、現在の `menu.sh` における `[y/N]` プロンプトは `_menu_forget` 内の2箇所と `_menu_cleanup` 内の1箇所です。これらが対象であることを確認済みですが、仕様書から読み取れないため、修正箇所を列挙しておくと実装者の迷いが減ります。
- `gum_required_hint` の i18n キーは `install.sh` の gum チェック後に表示するメッセージとして使われると思われますが、`install.sh` は現在 `t()` 関数を使っておらず、直接 `echo` で書かれています。`install.sh` で `i18n.sh` を source するのか、それとも新キーは `lib/gum.sh` 内から呼ぶのかが不明です。

toolUseResult.prompt:
以下の仕様書をレビューしてください。レビュー観点は「この仕様書に基づいて実装を開始できるか」です。

## レビュー基準

1. **Clarity（明確さ）** — 曖昧な表現・解釈が分かれる箇所はないか
2. **Verifiability（検証可能性）** — 各要件が「完了したか否か」を判断できるか
3. **Completeness（完全性）** — 実装に必要な情報が揃っているか
4. **Consistency（一貫性）** — 内部矛盾はないか

## 仕様書

```markdown
# Design: Interactive Wizard UI (gum-based)

**Date:** 2026-03-13
**Status:** Approved

---

## Overview

Redesign the `cw setup` / `cw new` flow from a linear 7-step prompt sequence into a 3-phase interactive wizard using [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum). Apply gum-based UI components across all commands for consistent, visually rich interactions. Maintain a fallback to the existing `read`-based UI when gum is not installed.

---

## Problem Statement

Current pain points identified:

1. **Directory selection is repetitive** — adding directories one by one with no visible running list makes the flow confusing.
2. **7-step sequence feels long** — no visible progress indicator; users don't know how far along they are.
3. **Weak input validation** — invalid paths are not flagged immediately at input time.
4. **Unclear next action after completion** — setup ends with "run `cw`" but does not offer to launch automatically.

---

## Solution

### Core: 3-Phase Wizard (`cw setup` / `cw new`)

Replace the 7-step linear flow with 3 named phases:

#### Phase 1 — Basic Info
- `gum input` for workspace name (pre-filled with directory basename for `cw setup`)
- `gum input` for description (optional)
- Phase header shows `Phase 1/3: Basic Info`

#### Phase 2 — Directories
- Display a live "Added so far" list before each prompt
- Path input uses `gum_path_input` (see Architecture) with the following tool-combination behavior:
  - gum + fzf both available: `gum input` for typing; pressing Tab launches fzf to browse
  - gum only: `gum input` (type path directly)
  - fzf only: `select_dir_with_fzf` (existing behavior)
  - neither: `read -rep` fallback
- Immediate validation: if path does not exist, show colored warning and `gum confirm "Add anyway?"` (or `[y/N]` fallback)
- `gum input` for optional role label per directory
- Loop continues until user presses Esc or submits empty input
- At least one directory is not required (user can add later with `cw add-dir`)

#### Phase 3 — Confirm & Launch
- Summary box showing: name, description, directory count, workspace path, files to be created
- `gum choose` with three options:
  - `▶ Create & Launch Claude` — create workspace files then exec into `cw launch`
  - `Create only` — create files and exit (existing behavior)
  - `✕ Cancel` — abort without writing anything

### Global UI Upgrades (all commands)

| Current pattern | Replacement |
|----------------|-------------|
| `read -rep "... [y/N]: "` | `gum confirm "..."` |
| Processing steps (file write, skill install) | `gum spin --title "..."` wrapping the operation |
| `warn "..."` / `error "..."` output | `gum style` with foreground color + border for errors |
| `cw add-dir` path prompt | `gum input` + fzf, same validation as Phase 2 |
| `cw forget` confirmation | `gum confirm` with destructive-action styling |

---

## Architecture

### New file: `lib/gum.sh`

Central gum abstraction layer. Provides wrapper functions that:
- Call gum when available (`command -v gum`)
- Fall back to existing `read`/`echo` patterns otherwise

Key functions:
```
gum_input <prompt> [default]      → gum input / read fallback
gum_confirm <message>             → gum confirm / [y/N] fallback
gum_spin <title> <command...>     → gum spin / direct execution fallback
                                     (bash 3.2 safe: uses "${@:2}" for command args)
gum_error <message>               → gum style (red border) / warn fallback
gum_choose <options...>           → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
                                     (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
gum_path_input <prompt> [default] → tool-combination-aware path selector:
                                     gum+fzf: gum input with Tab→fzf
                                     gum only: gum input
                                     fzf only: select_dir_with_fzf (existing)
                                     neither:  read -rep fallback
```

### Modified file: `lib/setup.sh`

- Replace 7-step sequence with `_setup_phase1`, `_setup_phase2`, `_setup_phase3` internal functions
- Call gum wrapper functions throughout
- Phase 3 result drives post-setup action (launch or exit)
- When "Create & Launch" is chosen, call `cmd_launch` directly (already sourced via `bin/cw` load order) — no subprocess, no double-launch risk
- `cw new` calls `cmd_setup` and then returns; Phase 3 inside `cmd_setup` handles the launch decision, so `cmd_new` does **not** add a separate `cmd_launch` call

### Modified file: `lib/utils.sh` / other command files

- Replace `read -rep "... [y/N]"` patterns with `gum_confirm`
- Replace processing steps with `gum_spin` wrappers where applicable

### Modified file: `install.sh`

- Add gum availability check after jq check
- If gum not found: suggest `brew install gum`, offer to install, or warn and continue
- Document gum as optional (graceful degradation)

---

## Fallback Behavior

When gum is not installed:
- `gum_input` → `read -rep`
- `gum_confirm` → `read -rep "... [y/N]: "`
- `gum_spin` → direct execution (no spinner)
- `gum_error` → existing `warn`/`error` functions
- `gum_choose` → numbered menu (inline numbered list via echo + read)

All existing behavior is preserved. The wrapper layer in `lib/gum.sh` is the single point of fallback logic.

---

## Out of Scope

- Full TUI dashboard (panel-based, real-time) — not in this iteration
- `cw web` UI changes — web UI is separate
- `cw list` / `cw info` output styling — low priority, can follow in a future iteration
- Windows/WSL support — not a stated requirement

---

## Success Criteria

- `cw setup` completes in 3 clear phases with visible phase indicator
- Directory list is visible throughout Phase 2
- Invalid paths show immediate colored warning
- Phase 3 offers "Create & Launch" that exits directly into Claude Code
- All `[y/N]` prompts replaced with `gum confirm` (when gum installed)
- Processing operations show spinner (when gum installed)
- Full functionality preserved when gum is not installed
- `install.sh` checks for gum and guides the user to install it

---

## Dependencies

- [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum) — optional, `brew install gum`
- No other new dependencies

---

## i18n Keys (New)

All new user-facing strings must be added to both `en` and `ja` blocks in `lib/i18n.sh`. Confirmed additions:

| Key | English | Japanese |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `phase1_header` | `Phase 1/3: Basic Info` | `フェーズ 1/3: 基本情報` |
| `phase2_header` | `Phase 2/3: Directories` | `フェーズ 2/3: ディレクトリ` |
| `phase3_header` | `Phase 3/3: Confirm` | `フェーズ 3/3: 確認` |
| `phase3_opt_launch` | `Create & Launch Claude` | `作成して Claude を起動` |
| `phase3_opt_create` | `Create only` | `作成のみ` |
| `phase3_opt_cancel` | `Cancel` | `キャンセル` |
| `phase3_summary` | `Summary` | `内容確認` |
| `path_add_anyway` | `Path does not exist. Add anyway?` | `ディレクトリが存在しません。追加しますか?` |
| `dirs_added_so_far` | `Added so far:` | `追加済み:` |
| `gum_required_hint` | `Install gum for a richer UI: brew install gum` | `より良いUIのために gum をインストール: brew install gum` |

All Phase 1–3 display strings must go through `t "key"`. No hardcoded display strings allowed.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `lib/gum.sh` | Create — gum wrapper abstraction |
| `lib/setup.sh` | Modify — rewrite to 3-phase wizard |
| `lib/utils.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/menu.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/new.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/launch.sh` | No change needed — `cmd_launch` called directly from Phase 3 |
| `install.sh` | Modify — add gum availability check |
| `lib/i18n.sh` | Modify — add all keys listed in i18n Keys section above |
```

## プロジェクトの背景（参考情報）

- **プロジェクト**: claude-workspace (cw) — Claude Code マルチリポジトリ Workspace マネージャー
- **言語**: Bash (macOS bash 3.2+ 互換)
- **既存の依存**: `jq`, `claude` (必須), `fzf` (オプション)

## 出力形式

APPROVE または REJECT で始め、以下を含めてください：
1. 判定理由
2. 各基準のスコア (1-5)
3. 問題点があれば具体的な改善案（上位3件まで）

content.text:
コードベースを把握しました。仕様書のレビューを行います。

---

## APPROVE（条件付き）

判定理由: 仕様書は全体的に実装開始できる水準に達しています。既存コードとの整合性も概ね取れており、アーキテクチャの方針も明確です。ただし、実装者が迷う可能性のある曖昧な箇所が3点あり、着手前に明確化することを推奨します。

---

## 各基準スコア

| 基準 | スコア | 評価 |
|------|--------|------|
| Clarity（明確さ） | 3 / 5 | `gum+fzf` のTab連携の実装方法、Phase 3で Launch を選んだ後の `cmd_new` のフローが曖昧 |
| Verifiability（検証可能性） | 4 / 5 | Success Criteria は具体的でテスト可能。ただし「全 [y/N] プロンプトを置き換え」は対象ファイルの網羅性が未確認 |
| Completeness（完全性） | 4 / 5 | 必要な情報はほぼ揃っている。`gum_choose` のフォールバック実装例が一部矛盾あり（後述） |
| Consistency（一貫性） | 3 / 5 | `gum_choose` のフォールバック説明が2箇所で食い違っている、`menu.sh` の変更内容が不明確 |

---

## 問題点と改善案（上位3件）

### 1. `gum_choose` フォールバックの説明が矛盾している

**該当箇所（2箇所）:**

Architecture セクション:
```
gum_choose <options...> → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
                           (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
```

Fallback Behavior セクション:
```
gum_choose → numbered menu (inline numbered list via echo + read)
```

これ自体は一致しているように見えますが、Phase 3 の `gum choose` に渡す3つの選択肢（`phase3_opt_launch`, `phase3_opt_create`, `phase3_opt_cancel`）の **返り値の扱い** が仕様書に書かれていません。`gum choose` は選択された文字列をそのまま stdout に返すため、`cmd_setup` の Phase 3 関数内でその文字列を何と比較して分岐するのか（i18n キーの値と比較? 内部定数と比較?）が不明確です。

**改善案:** Phase 3 の選択肢の比較ロジックを明示すること。例:
```
gum_choose の戻り値を case 文で比較する。
比較対象は t "phase3_opt_launch" の展開値とする。
```

---

### 2. `gum+fzf` のTab連携の実装方法が未定義

**該当箇所:**
```
gum + fzf both available: gum input for typing; pressing Tab launches fzf to browse
```

`gum input` はターミナルの生のキー入力を受け取る UI コンポーネントであり、Tab キーのフックを外部スクリプトから制御する標準的な手段がありません。実際には `gum input` の `--timeout` や終了コードで Tab を検知する方法は存在せず、実装不可能な記述になっている可能性があります。

**改善案:** この挙動の実現方法を明示するか、実装上の制約として認め、`gum+fzf` の場合も fzf をデフォルトのパスブラウザとして起動する（gum input は表示テキストとして使用しない）などの代替仕様に修正すること。

---

### 3. `cw new` の Phase 3「Create & Launch」後のフローに潜在的な二重起動リスク

**該当箇所:**
```
cw new calls cmd_setup and then returns; Phase 3 inside cmd_setup handles the launch 
decision, so cmd_new does not add a separate cmd_launch call
```

現在の `lib/new.sh` の `cmd_new` には `cmd_setup "$ws_name"` の後に明示的な `cmd_launch` 呼び出しがありません（確認済み）。この点は仕様書の記述と一致しています。

ただし、`cmd_new` の中で `cd "$target_dir"` を実行した後に `cmd_setup` を呼ぶ実装になっており（現コード line 75-76）、Phase 3 の中で `cmd_launch` を呼ぶと、`cmd_launch` は `pwd` ベースで動作するため、`target_dir` が正しい `pwd` であることが前提になります。Phase 3 が実行されるのは `cmd_setup` 内部（= `cd` 後）であるため実際は問題ないはずですが、仕様書には **`cmd_setup` が `pwd` に依存することを前提とした呼び出しである**という制約が明記されていません。

**改善案:** `cmd_setup` の Phase 3 で `cmd_launch` を呼ぶ際、`pwd` ではなく `target_dir` 変数（`_setup_phase1/2/3` に受け渡された値）を明示的に使用することを仕様に記載すること。または `cmd_launch` のシグネチャを `cmd_launch [dir]` に拡張する方針を明記すること。

---

## その他の軽微な指摘（判定には影響しない）

- `menu.sh` が「Modify — replace confirm patterns」とだけ書かれているが、現在の `menu.sh` における `[y/N]` プロンプトは `_menu_forget` 内の2箇所と `_menu_cleanup` 内の1箇所です。これらが対象であることを確認済みですが、仕様書から読み取れないため、修正箇所を列挙しておくと実装者の迷いが減ります。
- `gum_required_hint` の i18n キーは `install.sh` の gum チェック後に表示するメッセージとして使われると思われますが、`install.sh` は現在 `t()` 関数を使っておらず、直接 `echo` で書かれています。`install.sh` で `i18n.sh` を source するのか、それとも新キーは `lib/gum.sh` 内から呼ぶのかが不明です。
APPROVE（条件付き）が出ました。3点を修正します。

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Design: Interactive Wizard UI (gum-based)

**Date:** 2026-03-13
**Status:** Approved

---

## Overview

Redesign the `cw setup` / `cw new` flow from a linear 7-step prompt sequence into a 3-phase interactive wizard using [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum). Apply gum-based UI components across all commands for consistent, visually rich interactions. Maintain a fallback to the existing `read`-based UI when gum is not installed.

---

## Problem Statement

Current pain points identified:

1. **Directory selection is repetitive** — adding directories one by one with no visible running list makes the flow confusing.
2. **7-step sequence feels long** — no visible progress indicator; users don't know how far along they are.
3. **Weak input validation** — invalid paths are not flagged immediately at input time.
4. **Unclear next action after completion** — setup ends with "run `cw`" but does not offer to launch automatically.

---

## Solution

### Core: 3-Phase Wizard (`cw setup` / `cw new`)

Replace the 7-step linear flow with 3 named phases:

#### Phase 1 — Basic Info
- `gum input` for workspace name (pre-filled with directory basename for `cw setup`)
- `gum input` for description (optional)
- Phase header shows `Phase 1/3: Basic Info`

#### Phase 2 — Directories
- Display a live "Added so far" list before each prompt
- Path input uses `gum_path_input` (see Architecture) with the following tool-combination behavior:
  - gum + fzf both available: `gum input` for typing; pressing Tab launches fzf to browse
  - gum only: `gum input` (type path directly)
  - fzf only: `select_dir_with_fzf` (existing behavior)
  - neither: `read -rep` fallback
- Immediate validation: if path does not exist, show colored warning and `gum confirm "Add anyway?"` (or `[y/N]` fallback)
- `gum input` for optional role label per directory
- Loop continues until user presses Esc or submits empty input
- At least one directory is not required (user can add later with `cw add-dir`)

#### Phase 3 — Confirm & Launch
- Summary box showing: name, description, directory count, workspace path, files to be created
- `gum choose` with three options:
  - `▶ Create & Launch Claude` — create workspace files then exec into `cw launch`
  - `Create only` — create files and exit (existing behavior)
  - `✕ Cancel` — abort without writing anything

### Global UI Upgrades (all commands)

| Current pattern | Replacement |
|----------------|-------------|
| `read -rep "... [y/N]: "` | `gum confirm "..."` |
| Processing steps (file write, skill install) | `gum spin --title "..."` wrapping the operation |
| `warn "..."` / `error "..."` output | `gum style` with foreground color + border for errors |
| `cw add-dir` path prompt | `gum input` + fzf, same validation as Phase 2 |
| `cw forget` confirmation | `gum confirm` with destructive-action styling |

---

## Architecture

### New file: `lib/gum.sh`

Central gum abstraction layer. Provides wrapper functions that:
- Call gum when available (`command -v gum`)
- Fall back to existing `read`/`echo` patterns otherwise

Key functions:
```
gum_input <prompt> [default]      → gum input / read fallback
gum_confirm <message>             → gum confirm / [y/N] fallback
gum_spin <title> <command...>     → gum spin / direct execution fallback
                                     (bash 3.2 safe: uses "${@:2}" for command args)
gum_error <message>               → gum style (red border) / warn fallback
gum_choose <options...>           → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
                                     (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
gum_path_input <prompt> [default] → tool-combination-aware path selector:
                                     gum+fzf: gum input with Tab→fzf
                                     gum only: gum input
                                     fzf only: select_dir_with_fzf (existing)
                                     neither:  read -rep fallback
```

### Modified file: `lib/setup.sh`

- Replace 7-step sequence with `_setup_phase1`, `_setup_phase2`, `_setup_phase3` internal functions
- Call gum wrapper functions throughout
- Phase 3 result drives post-setup action (launch or exit)
- When "Create & Launch" is chosen, call `cmd_launch` directly (already sourced via `bin/cw` load order) — no subprocess, no double-launch risk
- `cw new` calls `cmd_setup` and then returns; Phase 3 inside `cmd_setup` handles the launch decision, so `cmd_new` does **not** add a separate `cmd_launch` call

### Modified file: `lib/utils.sh` / other command files

- Replace `read -rep "... [y/N]"` patterns with `gum_confirm`
- Replace processing steps with `gum_spin` wrappers where applicable

### Modified file: `install.sh`

- Add gum availability check after jq check
- If gum not found: suggest `brew install gum`, offer to install, or warn and continue
- Document gum as optional (graceful degradation)

---

## Fallback Behavior

When gum is not installed:
- `gum_input` → `read -rep`
- `gum_confirm` → `read -rep "... [y/N]: "`
- `gum_spin` → direct execution (no spinner)
- `gum_error` → existing `warn`/`error` functions
- `gum_choose` → numbered menu (existing `_menu_numbered_pick` pattern)

All existing behavior is preserved. The wrapper layer in `lib/gum.sh` is the single point of fallback logic.

---

## Out of Scope

- Full TUI dashboard (panel-based, real-time) — not in this iteration
- `cw web` UI changes — web UI is separate
- `cw list` / `cw info` output styling — low priority, can follow in a future iteration
- Windows/WSL support — not a stated requirement

---

## Success Criteria

- `cw setup` completes in 3 clear phases with visible phase indicator
- Directory list is visible throughout Phase 2
- Invalid paths show immediate colored warning
- Phase 3 offers "Create & Launch" that exits directly into Claude Code
- All `[y/N]` prompts replaced with `gum confirm` (when gum installed)
- Processing operations show spinner (when gum installed)
- Full functionality preserved when gum is not installed
- `install.sh` checks for gum and guides the user to install it

---

## Dependencies

- [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum) — optional, `brew install gum`
- No other new dependencies

---

## i18n Keys (New)

All new user-facing strings must be added to both `en` and `ja` blocks in `lib/i18n.sh`. Confirmed additions:

| Key | English | Japanese |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `phase1_header` | `Phase 1/3: Basic Info` | `フェーズ 1/3: 基本情報` |
| `phase2_header` | `Phase 2/3: Directories` | `フェーズ 2/3: ディレクトリ` |
| `phase3_header` | `Phase 3/3: Confirm` | `フェーズ 3/3: 確認` |
| `phase3_opt_launch` | `Create & Launch Claude` | `作成して Claude を起動` |
| `phase3_opt_create` | `Create only` | `作成のみ` |
| `phase3_opt_cancel` | `Cancel` | `キャンセル` |
| `phase3_summary` | `Summary` | `内容確認` |
| `path_add_anyway` | `Path does not exist. Add anyway?` | `ディレクトリが存在しません。追加しますか?` |
| `dirs_added_so_far` | `Added so far:` | `追加済み:` |
| `gum_required_hint` | `Install gum for a richer UI: brew install gum` | `より良いUIのために gum をインストール: brew install gum` |

All Phase 1–3 display strings must go through `t "key"`. No hardcoded display strings allowed.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `lib/gum.sh` | Create — gum wrapper abstraction |
| `lib/setup.sh` | Modify — rewrite to 3-phase wizard |
| `lib/utils.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/menu.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/new.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/launch.sh` | No change needed — `cmd_launch` called directly from Phase 3 |
| `install.sh` | Modify — add gum availability check |
| `lib/i18n.sh` | Modify — add all keys listed in i18n Keys section above |

```

gum_spin <title> <command...>     → gum spin / direct execution fallback
                                     (bash 3.2 safe: uses "${@:2}" for command args)
gum_error <message>               → gum style (red border) / warn fallback
gum_choose <options...>           → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
                                     (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
gum_path_input <prompt> [default] → tool-combination-aware path selector:
                                     gum+fzf: gum input with Tab→fzf
                                     gum only: gum input

```

# Design: Interactive Wizard UI (gum-based)

**Date:** 2026-03-13
**Status:** Approved

---

## Overview

Redesign the `cw setup` / `cw new` flow from a linear 7-step prompt sequence into a 3-phase interactive wizard using [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum). Apply gum-based UI components across all commands for consistent, visually rich interactions. Maintain a fallback to the existing `read`-based UI when gum is not installed.

---

## Problem Statement

Current pain points identified:

1. **Directory selection is repetitive** — adding directories one by one with no visible running list makes the flow confusing.
2. **7-step sequence feels long** — no visible progress indicator; users don't know how far along they are.
3. **Weak input validation** — invalid paths are not flagged immediately at input time.
4. **Unclear next action after completion** — setup ends with "run `cw`" but does not offer to launch automatically.

---

## Solution

### Core: 3-Phase Wizard (`cw setup` / `cw new`)

Replace the 7-step linear flow with 3 named phases:

#### Phase 1 — Basic Info
- `gum input` for workspace name (pre-filled with directory basename for `cw setup`)
- `gum input` for description (optional)
- Phase header shows `Phase 1/3: Basic Info`

#### Phase 2 — Directories
- Display a live "Added so far" list before each prompt
- Path input uses `gum_path_input` (see Architecture) with the following tool-combination behavior:
  - gum + fzf both available: fzf for directory browsing (gum used for styling the prompt header only)
  - gum only: `gum input` (type path directly; no Tab-fzf integration — not feasible with gum input)
  - fzf only: `select_dir_with_fzf` (existing behavior)
  - neither: `read -rep` fallback
- Immediate validation: if path does not exist, show colored warning and `gum confirm "Add anyway?"` (or `[y/N]` fallback)
- `gum input` for optional role label per directory
- Loop continues until user presses Esc or submits empty input
- At least one directory is not required (user can add later with `cw add-dir`)

#### Phase 3 — Confirm & Launch
- Summary box showing: name, description, directory count, workspace path, files to be created
- `gum_choose` with three options (comparison uses internal constants, not i18n values):
  - `LAUNCH` → display label `t "phase3_opt_launch"` — create workspace files then call `cmd_launch` with explicit `target_dir`
  - `CREATE` → display label `t "phase3_opt_create"` — create files and exit (existing behavior)
  - `CANCEL` → display label `t "phase3_opt_cancel"` — abort without writing anything
- `gum_choose` returns the internal constant (e.g. `LAUNCH`); Phase 3 uses `case "$choice" in LAUNCH) ...` for branching
- When "LAUNCH" is chosen, `cmd_launch` is called with the workspace directory passed explicitly (not relying on `pwd`)

### Global UI Upgrades (all commands)

| Current pattern | Replacement |
|----------------|-------------|
| `read -rep "... [y/N]: "` | `gum confirm "..."` |
| Processing steps (file write, skill install) | `gum spin --title "..."` wrapping the operation |
| `warn "..."` / `error "..."` output | `gum style` with foreground color + border for errors |
| `cw add-dir` path prompt | `gum input` + fzf, same validation as Phase 2 |
| `cw forget` confirmation | `gum confirm` with destructive-action styling |

---

## Architecture

### New file: `lib/gum.sh`

Central gum abstraction layer. Provides wrapper functions that:
- Call gum when available (`command -v gum`)
- Fall back to existing `read`/`echo` patterns otherwise

Key functions:
```
gum_input <prompt> [default]      → gum input / read fallback
gum_confirm <message>             → gum confirm / [y/N] fallback
gum_spin <title> <command...>     → gum spin / direct execution fallback
                                     (bash 3.2 safe: uses "${@:2}" for command args)
gum_error <message>               → gum style (red border) / warn fallback
gum_choose <options...>           → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
                                     (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
gum_path_input <prompt> [default] → tool-combination-aware path selector:
                                     gum+fzf: gum input with Tab→fzf
                                     gum only: gum input
                                     fzf only: select_dir_with_fzf (existing)
                                     neither:  read -rep fallback
```

### Modified file: `lib/setup.sh`

- Replace 7-step sequence with `_setup_phase1`, `_setup_phase2`, `_setup_phase3` internal functions
- Call gum wrapper functions throughout
- Phase 3 result drives post-setup action (launch or exit)
- When "Create & Launch" is chosen, call `cmd_launch` directly (already sourced via `bin/cw` load order) — no subprocess, no double-launch risk
- `cw new` calls `cmd_setup` and then returns; Phase 3 inside `cmd_setup` handles the launch decision, so `cmd_new` does **not** add a separate `cmd_launch` call

### Modified file: `lib/utils.sh` / other command files

- Replace `read -rep "... [y/N]"` patterns with `gum_confirm`
- Replace processing steps with `gum_spin` wrappers where applicable

### Modified file: `install.sh`

- Add gum availability check after jq check
- If gum not found: suggest `brew install gum`, offer to install, or warn and continue
- Document gum as optional (graceful degradation)

---

## Fallback Behavior

When gum is not installed:
- `gum_input` → `read -rep`
- `gum_confirm` → `read -rep "... [y/N]: "`
- `gum_spin` → direct execution (no spinner)
- `gum_error` → existing `warn`/`error` functions
- `gum_choose` → numbered menu (existing `_menu_numbered_pick` pattern)

All existing behavior is preserved. The wrapper layer in `lib/gum.sh` is the single point of fallback logic.

---

## Out of Scope

- Full TUI dashboard (panel-based, real-time) — not in this iteration
- `cw web` UI changes — web UI is separate
- `cw list` / `cw info` output styling — low priority, can follow in a future iteration
- Windows/WSL support — not a stated requirement

---

## Success Criteria

- `cw setup` completes in 3 clear phases with visible phase indicator
- Directory list is visible throughout Phase 2
- Invalid paths show immediate colored warning
- Phase 3 offers "Create & Launch" that exits directly into Claude Code
- All `[y/N]` prompts replaced with `gum confirm` (when gum installed)
- Processing operations show spinner (when gum installed)
- Full functionality preserved when gum is not installed
- `install.sh` checks for gum and guides the user to install it

---

## Dependencies

- [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum) — optional, `brew install gum`
- No other new dependencies

---

## i18n Keys (New)

All new user-facing strings must be added to both `en` and `ja` blocks in `lib/i18n.sh`. Confirmed additions:

| Key | English | Japanese |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `phase1_header` | `Phase 1/3: Basic Info` | `フェーズ 1/3: 基本情報` |
| `phase2_header` | `Phase 2/3: Directories` | `フェーズ 2/3: ディレクトリ` |
| `phase3_header` | `Phase 3/3: Confirm` | `フェーズ 3/3: 確認` |
| `phase3_opt_launch` | `Create & Launch Claude` | `作成して Claude を起動` |
| `phase3_opt_create` | `Create only` | `作成のみ` |
| `phase3_opt_cancel` | `Cancel` | `キャンセル` |
| `phase3_summary` | `Summary` | `内容確認` |
| `path_add_anyway` | `Path does not exist. Add anyway?` | `ディレクトリが存在しません。追加しますか?` |
| `dirs_added_so_far` | `Added so far:` | `追加済み:` |
| `gum_required_hint` | `Install gum for a richer UI: brew install gum` | `より良いUIのために gum をインストール: brew install gum` |

All Phase 1–3 display strings must go through `t "key"`. No hardcoded display strings allowed.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `lib/gum.sh` | Create — gum wrapper abstraction |
| `lib/setup.sh` | Modify — rewrite to 3-phase wizard |
| `lib/utils.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/menu.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/new.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/launch.sh` | No change needed — `cmd_launch` called directly from Phase 3 |
| `install.sh` | Modify — add gum availability check |
| `lib/i18n.sh` | Modify — add all keys listed in i18n Keys section above |

-                                     gum+fzf: gum input with Tab→fzf
-                                     gum only: gum input
+                                     gum+fzf: fzf browse (gum for prompt styling only)
+                                     gum only: gum input (type path directly)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Design: Interactive Wizard UI (gum-based)

**Date:** 2026-03-13
**Status:** Approved

---

## Overview

Redesign the `cw setup` / `cw new` flow from a linear 7-step prompt sequence into a 3-phase interactive wizard using [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum). Apply gum-based UI components across all commands for consistent, visually rich interactions. Maintain a fallback to the existing `read`-based UI when gum is not installed.

---

## Problem Statement

Current pain points identified:

1. **Directory selection is repetitive** — adding directories one by one with no visible running list makes the flow confusing.
2. **7-step sequence feels long** — no visible progress indicator; users don't know how far along they are.
3. **Weak input validation** — invalid paths are not flagged immediately at input time.
4. **Unclear next action after completion** — setup ends with "run `cw`" but does not offer to launch automatically.

---

## Solution

### Core: 3-Phase Wizard (`cw setup` / `cw new`)

Replace the 7-step linear flow with 3 named phases:

#### Phase 1 — Basic Info
- `gum input` for workspace name (pre-filled with directory basename for `cw setup`)
- `gum input` for description (optional)
- Phase header shows `Phase 1/3: Basic Info`

#### Phase 2 — Directories
- Display a live "Added so far" list before each prompt
- Path input uses `gum_path_input` (see Architecture) with the following tool-combination behavior:
  - gum + fzf both available: fzf for directory browsing (gum used for styling the prompt header only)
  - gum only: `gum input` (type path directly; no Tab-fzf integration — not feasible with gum input)
  - fzf only: `select_dir_with_fzf` (existing behavior)
  - neither: `read -rep` fallback
- Immediate validation: if path does not exist, show colored warning and `gum confirm "Add anyway?"` (or `[y/N]` fallback)
- `gum input` for optional role label per directory
- Loop continues until user presses Esc or submits empty input
- At least one directory is not required (user can add later with `cw add-dir`)

#### Phase 3 — Confirm & Launch
- Summary box showing: name, description, directory count, workspace path, files to be created
- `gum_choose` with three options (comparison uses internal constants, not i18n values):
  - `LAUNCH` → display label `t "phase3_opt_launch"` — create workspace files then call `cmd_launch` with explicit `target_dir`
  - `CREATE` → display label `t "phase3_opt_create"` — create files and exit (existing behavior)
  - `CANCEL` → display label `t "phase3_opt_cancel"` — abort without writing anything
- `gum_choose` returns the internal constant (e.g. `LAUNCH`); Phase 3 uses `case "$choice" in LAUNCH) ...` for branching
- When "LAUNCH" is chosen, `cmd_launch` is called with the workspace directory passed explicitly (not relying on `pwd`)

### Global UI Upgrades (all commands)

| Current pattern | Replacement |
|----------------|-------------|
| `read -rep "... [y/N]: "` | `gum confirm "..."` |
| Processing steps (file write, skill install) | `gum spin --title "..."` wrapping the operation |
| `warn "..."` / `error "..."` output | `gum style` with foreground color + border for errors |
| `cw add-dir` path prompt | `gum input` + fzf, same validation as Phase 2 |
| `cw forget` confirmation | `gum confirm` with destructive-action styling |

---

## Architecture

### New file: `lib/gum.sh`

Central gum abstraction layer. Provides wrapper functions that:
- Call gum when available (`command -v gum`)
- Fall back to existing `read`/`echo` patterns otherwise

Key functions:
```
gum_input <prompt> [default]      → gum input / read fallback
gum_confirm <message>             → gum confirm / [y/N] fallback
gum_spin <title> <command...>     → gum spin / direct execution fallback
                                     (bash 3.2 safe: uses "${@:2}" for command args)
gum_error <message>               → gum style (red border) / warn fallback
gum_choose <options...>           → gum choose / numbered menu fallback
                                     (fallback: inline numbered list via echo + read)
gum_path_input <prompt> [default] → tool-combination-aware path selector:
                                     gum+fzf: fzf browse (gum for prompt styling only)
                                     gum only: gum input (type path directly)
                                     fzf only: select_dir_with_fzf (existing)
                                     neither:  read -rep fallback
```

### Modified file: `lib/setup.sh`

- Replace 7-step sequence with `_setup_phase1`, `_setup_phase2`, `_setup_phase3` internal functions
- Call gum wrapper functions throughout
- Phase 3 result drives post-setup action (launch or exit)
- When "LAUNCH" is chosen, call `cmd_launch` with `target_dir` passed explicitly — do NOT rely on `pwd` (bash's `pwd` state is not guaranteed to match `target_dir` after subshell calls)
- `cmd_launch` will need to accept an optional `[dir]` argument; if provided, it `cd`s to that dir instead of using `pwd`
- `cw new` calls `cmd_setup` and then returns; Phase 3 inside `cmd_setup` handles the launch decision, so `cmd_new` does **not** add a separate `cmd_launch` call

### Modified file: `lib/utils.sh` / other command files

- Replace `read -rep "... [y/N]"` patterns with `gum_confirm`
- Replace processing steps with `gum_spin` wrappers where applicable

### Modified file: `install.sh`

- Add gum availability check after jq check
- If gum not found: suggest `brew install gum`, offer to install, or warn and continue
- Document gum as optional (graceful degradation)

---

## Fallback Behavior

When gum is not installed:
- `gum_input` → `read -rep`
- `gum_confirm` → `read -rep "... [y/N]: "`
- `gum_spin` → direct execution (no spinner)
- `gum_error` → existing `warn`/`error` functions
- `gum_choose` → numbered menu (existing `_menu_numbered_pick` pattern)

All existing behavior is preserved. The wrapper layer in `lib/gum.sh` is the single point of fallback logic.

---

## Out of Scope

- Full TUI dashboard (panel-based, real-time) — not in this iteration
- `cw web` UI changes — web UI is separate
- `cw list` / `cw info` output styling — low priority, can follow in a future iteration
- Windows/WSL support — not a stated requirement

---

## Success Criteria

- `cw setup` completes in 3 clear phases with visible phase indicator
- Directory list is visible throughout Phase 2
- Invalid paths show immediate colored warning
- Phase 3 offers "Create & Launch" that exits directly into Claude Code
- All `[y/N]` prompts replaced with `gum confirm` (when gum installed)
- Processing operations show spinner (when gum installed)
- Full functionality preserved when gum is not installed
- `install.sh` checks for gum and guides the user to install it

---

## Dependencies

- [gum](https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum) — optional, `brew install gum`
- No other new dependencies

---

## i18n Keys (New)

All new user-facing strings must be added to both `en` and `ja` blocks in `lib/i18n.sh`. Confirmed additions:

| Key | English | Japanese |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `phase1_header` | `Phase 1/3: Basic Info` | `フェーズ 1/3: 基本情報` |
| `phase2_header` | `Phase 2/3: Directories` | `フェーズ 2/3: ディレクトリ` |
| `phase3_header` | `Phase 3/3: Confirm` | `フェーズ 3/3: 確認` |
| `phase3_opt_launch` | `Create & Launch Claude` | `作成して Claude を起動` |
| `phase3_opt_create` | `Create only` | `作成のみ` |
| `phase3_opt_cancel` | `Cancel` | `キャンセル` |
| `phase3_summary` | `Summary` | `内容確認` |
| `path_add_anyway` | `Path does not exist. Add anyway?` | `ディレクトリが存在しません。追加しますか?` |
| `dirs_added_so_far` | `Added so far:` | `追加済み:` |
| `gum_required_hint` | `Install gum for a richer UI: brew install gum` | `より良いUIのために gum をインストール: brew install gum` |

All Phase 1–3 display strings must go through `t "key"`. No hardcoded display strings allowed.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `lib/gum.sh` | Create — gum wrapper abstraction |
| `lib/setup.sh` | Modify — rewrite to 3-phase wizard |
| `lib/utils.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/menu.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/new.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/launch.sh` | No change needed — `cmd_launch` called directly from Phase 3 |
| `install.sh` | Modify — add gum availability check |
| `lib/i18n.sh` | Modify — add all keys listed in i18n Keys section above |

| `lib/menu.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
| `lib/new.sh` | Modify — replace confirm patterns |
-| `lib/launch.sh` | No change needed — `cmd_launch` called directly from Phase 3 |
+| `lib/launch.sh` | Modify — add optional `[dir]` argument to `cmd_launch` for Phase 3 direct call |
| `install.sh` | Modify — add gum availability check |
| `lib/i18n.sh` | Modify — add all keys listed in i18n Keys section above |

以下の仕様書をレビューしてください。「この仕様書に基づいて実装を開始できるか」の観点でAPPROVE/REJECTを判定してください。

## レビュー基準

1. **Clarity（明確さ）** — 曖昧な表現・解釈が分かれる箇所はないか
2. **Verifiability（検証可能性）** — 各要件が「完了したか否か」を判断できるか
3. **Completeness（完全性）** — 実装に必要な情報が揃っているか
4. **Consistency（一貫性）** — 内部矛盾はないか

## 仕様書

ファイルを読んでください: `/Users/yurikamo/Developer/github/claude-workspace/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-interactive-wizard-design.md`

## プロジェクト背景

- Bash (macOS bash 3.2+ 互換), 既存依存: `jq`, `claude` (必須), `fzf` (オプション)
- 新依存: `gum` (オプション, フォールバックあり)

## 出力形式

APPROVE または REJECT で始め、判定理由と各基準スコア(1-5)を含めてください。APPROVEの場合は軽微な指摘のみ許容、REJECTの場合は必ず修正が必要な問題点を列挙してください。

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# launch.sh - cw launch command (Launch Claude Code)
     3→
     4→cmd_launch() {
     5→  require_jq
     6→
     7→  local target_dir
     8→  target_dir="$(pwd)"
     9→
    10→  # If workspace name is provided as argument, find the directory
    11→  if [[ -n "${1:-}" ]]; then
    12→    local found
    13→    found=$(_find_workspace_by_name "$1")
    14→    if [[ -z "$found" ]]; then
    15→      error "$(t "workspace_not_found"): $1"
    16→      echo "  Registered workspaces: cw list"
    17→      exit 1
    18→    fi
    19→    target_dir="$found"
    20→  fi
    21→
    22→  if ! is_workspace "$target_dir"; then
    23→    error "$(t "workspace_not_found"): $target_dir"
    24→    echo "  $(dim "$(t "launch_here")")"
    25→    exit 1
    26→  fi
    27→
    28→  local ws_file="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE"
    29→  local ws_name
    30→  ws_name=$(ws_get "$ws_file" '.name')
    31→
    32→  echo ""
    33→  echo "$(bold "$(t "workspace_launch"): $ws_name")"
    34→
    35→  # Get registered directories
    36→  local dir_count
    37→  dir_count=$(jq '.dirs | length' "$ws_file")
    38→
    39→  local add_dir_flags=()
    40→  local valid_count=0
    41→
    42→  for ((i=0; i<dir_count; i++)); do
    43→    local dir
    44→    dir=$(jq -r ".dirs[$i].path" "$ws_file")
    45→    local role
    46→    role=$(jq -r ".dirs[$i].role // \"\"" "$ws_file")
    47→
    48→    if [[ -d "$dir" ]]; then
    49→      add_dir_flags+=("--add-dir" "$dir")
    50→      local label="${role:-$(basename "$dir")}"
    51→      info "  + $label  $(dim "$dir")"
    52→      valid_count=$((valid_count + 1))
    53→    else
    54→      warn "  $(t "skip_not_exist"): $dir"
    55→    fi
    56→  done
    57→
    58→  echo ""
    59→
    60→  if [[ $valid_count -eq 0 ]]; then
    61→    warn "$(t "launch_valid_dirs")."
    62→    warn "$(t "launch_use_add_dir")."
    63→  fi
    64→
    65→  # Check if Claude Code exists
    66→  if ! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
    67→    error "$(t "error_claude_not_found")"
    68→    echo "  Install: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
    69→    exit 1
    70→  fi
    71→
    72→  echo "  $(dim "claude ${add_dir_flags[*]+"${add_dir_flags[*]}"}")"
    73→  echo ""
    74→
    75→  # Update last_used (only if registry exists)
    76→  registry_touch "$target_dir"
    77→
    78→  # Launch Claude Code in workspace directory
    79→  cd "$target_dir"
    80→  exec claude "${add_dir_flags[@]+"${add_dir_flags[@]}"}"
    81→}
    82→
    83→# Find workspace directory by name
    84→# Prioritize registry, then search filesystem
    85→_find_workspace_by_name() {
    86→  local name="$1"
    87→
    88→  # Check global registry first (use registry.sh function)
    89→  local found
    90→  found=$(registry_get_by_name "$name")
    91→  [[ -n "$found" ]] && { echo "$found"; return; }
    92→
    93→  # Fallback: Search under WorkingProjects
    94→  local search_base="${WORKING_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/WorkingProjects}"
    95→  if [[ -d "$search_base" ]]; then
    96→    while IFS= read -r ws_file; do
    97→      local ws_name
    98→      ws_name=$(jq -r '.name' "$ws_file" 2>/dev/null)
    99→      if [[ "$ws_name" == "$name" ]]; then
   100→        dirname "$ws_file"
   101→        return
   102→      fi
   103→    done < <(find "$search_base" -maxdepth 2 -name ".workspace.json" 2>/dev/null)
   104→  fi
   105→}
   106→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# setup.sh - cw setup command
     3→
     4→# Arguments: [prefill_name]
     5→#   prefill_name ... passed when called from cw new to skip name input
     6→cmd_setup() {
     7→  require_jq
     8→
     9→  local prefill_name="${1:-}"
    10→
    11→  local target_dir
    12→  target_dir="$(pwd)"
    13→
    14→  # Check if already set up as workspace
    15→  if is_workspace "$target_dir"; then
    16→    warn "$(t "workspace_already_setup")"
    17→    local existing_name
    18→    existing_name=$(ws_get "$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE" '.name')
    19→    echo "  $(t "workspace_name"): $(bold "$existing_name")"
    20→    echo ""
    21→    read -rep "  $(t "re_setup") [y/N]: " confirm
    22→    [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]] || { info "$(t "cancel")"; exit 0; }
    23→    echo ""
    24→  fi
    25→
    26→  echo ""
    27→  echo "$(bold "Claude Workspace Setup")"
    28→  echo "$(dim "$(t "directory"): $target_dir")"
    29→  echo ""
    30→
    31→  # ──────────────────────────────
    32→  # Step 1: Workspace name
    33→  # ──────────────────────────────
    34→  step "Step 1: $(t "setup_step1")"
    35→  local ws_name
    36→  if [[ -n "$prefill_name" ]]; then
    37→    # Called from cw new, name already set
    38→    ws_name="$prefill_name"
    39→    success "$(t "workspace_name"): $ws_name"
    40→  else
    41→    local default_name
    42→    default_name="$(basename "$target_dir")"
    43→    read -rep "  $(t "workspace_name") [${default_name}]: " ws_name
    44→    ws_name="${ws_name:-$default_name}"
    45→    success "$(t "workspace_name"): $ws_name"
    46→  fi
    47→
    48→  # ──────────────────────────────
    49→  # Step 2: Description
    50→  # ──────────────────────────────
    51→  step "Step 2: $(t "setup_step2")"
    52→  read -rep "  $(t "workspace_desc"): " ws_description
    53→  ws_description="${ws_description:-}"
    54→
    55→  # ──────────────────────────────
    56→  # Step 3: Add target directories
    57→  # ──────────────────────────────
    58→  step "Step 3: $(t "setup_step3")"
    59→  echo "  $(dim "$(t "setup_path_hint")")"
    60→  echo "  $(dim "Example: ~/repos/my-project")"
    61→  echo ""
    62→
    63→  local dirs=()
    64→  local dir_roles=()
    65→
    66→  while true; do
    67→    local raw_path
    68→    raw_path=$(select_dir_with_fzf "$(t "select_dir_prompt")")
    69→    [[ -z "$raw_path" ]] && break
    70→
    71→    local expanded_path="$raw_path"
    72→
    73→    if [[ ! -d "$expanded_path" ]]; then
    74→      warn "$(t "dir_not_found"): $expanded_path"
    75→      read -rep "  $(t "add_anyway") [y/N]: " force
    76→      [[ "$force" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]] || continue
    77→    fi
    78→
    79→    # Role label (optional)
    80→    read -rep "  $(t "dir_add_role"): " role
    81→    role="${role:-}"
    82→
    83→    dirs+=("$expanded_path")
    84→    dir_roles+=("$role")
    85→    success "$(t "dir_added"): $expanded_path $([ -n "$role" ] && echo "(${role})")"
    86→  done
    87→
    88→  if [[ ${#dirs[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    89→    warn "No directories specified. Use cw add-dir later to add."
    90→  fi
    91→
    92→  # ──────────────────────────────
    93→  # Step 4: Generate workspace.json
    94→  # ──────────────────────────────
    95→  step "Step 4: $(t "setup_step4")"
    96→
    97→  local ws_file="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE"
    98→  local created_at
    99→  created_at=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
   100→
   101→  # Build JSON
   102→  local dirs_json="[]"
   103→  for i in "${!dirs[@]}"; do
   104→    local dir="${dirs[$i]}"
   105→    local role="${dir_roles[$i]}"
   106→    dirs_json=$(echo "$dirs_json" | jq \
   107→      --arg path "$dir" \
   108→      --arg role "$role" \
   109→      '. += [{"path": $path, "role": $role}]')
   110→  done
   111→
   112→  jq -n \
   113→    --arg name "$ws_name" \
   114→    --arg description "$ws_description" \
   115→    --arg created_at "$created_at" \
   116→    --arg workspace_path "$target_dir" \
   117→    --argjson dirs "$dirs_json" \
   118→    '{
   119→      name: $name,
   120→      description: $description,
   121→      workspace_path: $workspace_path,
   122→      created_at: $created_at,
   123→      dirs: $dirs
   124→    }' > "$ws_file"
   125→
   126→  success "$(t "created"): $ws_file"
   127→
   128→  # ──────────────────────────────
   129→  # Step 5: Generate CLAUDE.md
   130→  # ──────────────────────────────
   131→  step "Step 5: $(t "setup_step5")"
   132→
   133→  local claude_md="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_CLAUDE_MD"
   134→  _generate_workspace_claude_md "$claude_md" "$ws_name" "$ws_description" "$ws_file"
   135→  success "$(t "created"): $claude_md"
   136→
   137→  # ──────────────────────────────
   138→  # Step 6: Install skills to .claude/skills/
   139→  # ──────────────────────────────
   140→  step "Step 6: $(t "setup_step6")"
   141→
   142→  if [[ -d "$CW_SKILLS_DIR" ]]; then
   143→    local ws_skills_dir="$target_dir/.claude/skills"
   144→    mkdir -p "$ws_skills_dir"
   145→    for skill_dir in "$CW_SKILLS_DIR"/*/; do
   146→      [[ -d "$skill_dir" ]] || continue
   147→      local skill_name
   148→      skill_name="$(basename "$skill_dir")"
   149→      cp -R "$skill_dir" "$ws_skills_dir/$skill_name"
   150→    done
   151→    success "$(t "setup_skills_installed"): .claude/skills/"
   152→  fi
   153→
   154→  # ──────────────────────────────
   155→  # Step 7: Register to global registry
   156→  # ──────────────────────────────
   157→  step "Step 7: $(t "setup_step7")"
   158→  registry_add "$ws_name" "$target_dir"
   159→  success "$(t "registered"): ~/.claude-workspace/registry.json"
   160→
   161→  # ──────────────────────────────
   162→  # Complete
   163→  # ──────────────────────────────
   164→  echo ""
   165→  echo "$(green "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")"
   166→  echo "$(green "  $(t "setup_complete")")"
   167→  echo "$(green "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━")"
   168→  echo ""
   169→  echo "  $(t "setup_run")"
   170→  echo "  $(bold "  cw")"
   171→  echo ""
   172→}
   173→
   174→# Generate workspace CLAUDE.md template
   175→# Arguments: <claude_md_path> <ws_name> <ws_description> <ws_file_path>
   176→_generate_workspace_claude_md() {
   177→  local claude_md="$1"
   178→  local ws_name="$2"
   179→  local ws_description="$3"
   180→  local ws_file="$4"
   181→
   182→  # Build Linked Repositories table rows
   183→  local dirs_table=""
   184→  if [[ -f "$ws_file" ]]; then
   185→    local count
   186→    count=$(jq '.dirs | length' "$ws_file")
   187→    for ((i=0; i<count; i++)); do
   188→      local path role
   189→      path=$(jq -r ".dirs[$i].path" "$ws_file")
   190→      role=$(jq -r ".dirs[$i].role // \"\"" "$ws_file")
   191→      local label="${role:-$(basename "$path")}"
   192→      dirs_table="${dirs_table}| ${label} | \`${path}\` |"$'\n'
   193→    done
   194→  fi
   195→
   196→  local created_at
   197→  created_at=$(date "+%Y-%m-%d")
   198→
   199→  # ${ws_name,,} requires bash 4+, use tr for macOS bash 3.2 compatibility
   200→  local ws_name_lower
   201→  ws_name_lower=$(echo "$ws_name" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr ' ' '-')
   202→
   203→  # Use printf to avoid heredoc variable expansion issues
   204→  printf '# Workspace: %s\n\n> %s\n\nCreated: %s\n\n---\n\n## Linked Repositories\n\n| Role | Path |\n|------|------|\n%s\n## Workflow Rules\n\n- **REQUIRED**: Use the `workspace-task-manager` skill for task decomposition and management\n  - Decompose workspace goals into tasks before starting implementation\n  - Each task gets a `YYYY-MM-DD-{task-name}/` directory under this workspace\n  - Follow the superpowers workflow (brainstorming → writing-plans → execute) per task\n  - Save design docs and plans into the task directory, not `docs/plans/`\n- Branch naming convention: `feature/ws-%s-*`\n\n## Current Tasks\n\n| Status | Task | Directory | Priority |\n|--------|------|-----------|----------|\n| | (Use workspace-task-manager skill to populate) | | |\n\n## Notes\n\nRecord notes and design decisions here.\n' \
   205→    "$ws_name" \
   206→    "${ws_description:-Describe this workspace here.}" \
   207→    "$created_at" \
   208→    "$dirs_table" \
   209→    "$ws_name_lower" \
   210→    > "$claude_md"
   211→}
   212→
   213→# cw add-dir command
   214→cmd_add_dir() {
   215→  require_jq
   216→
   217→  local target_dir
   218→  target_dir="$(pwd)"
   219→
   220→  if ! is_workspace "$target_dir"; then
   221→    error "$(t "workspace_not_found"). Run cw setup first."
   222→    exit 1
   223→  fi
   224→
   225→  local ws_file="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_FILE"
   226→  local ws_name
   227→  ws_name=$(ws_get "$ws_file" '.name')
   228→
   229→  echo ""
   230→  echo "$(bold "$(t "add_directory"): $ws_name")"
   231→  echo ""
   232→
   233→  local new_path="${1:-}"
   234→  if [[ -z "$new_path" ]]; then
   235→    new_path=$(select_dir_with_fzf "$(t "select_dir_prompt")")
   236→  fi
   237→  [[ -z "$new_path" ]] && { info "$(t "cancel")"; exit 0; }
   238→
   239→  local expanded_path
   240→  expanded_path=$(expand_path "$new_path")
   241→
   242→  if [[ ! -d "$expanded_path" ]]; then
   243→    warn "$(t "dir_not_found"): $expanded_path"
   244→    read -rep "  $(t "add_anyway") [y/N]: " force
   245→    [[ "$force" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]] || exit 0
   246→  fi
   247→
   248→  # Check if already registered
   249→  if jq -e --arg p "$expanded_path" '.dirs[] | select(.path == $p)' "$ws_file" &>/dev/null; then
   250→    warn "$(t "already_exists"): $expanded_path"
   251→    exit 0
   252→  fi
   253→
   254→  read -rep "  $(t "dir_add_role"): " role
   255→
   256→  # Add to workspace.json
   257→  ws_set "$ws_file" \
   258→    --arg path "$expanded_path" \
   259→    --arg role "${role:-}" \
   260→    '.dirs += [{"path": $path, "role": $role}]'
   261→
   262→  # Update CLAUDE.md (regenerate Linked Repositories table)
   263→  local claude_md="$target_dir/$WORKSPACE_CLAUDE_MD"
   264→  local ws_description
   265→  ws_description=$(ws_get "$ws_file" '.description')
   266→  _generate_workspace_claude_md "$claude_md" "$ws_name" "$ws_description" "$ws_file"
   267→
   268→  success "$(t "dir_added"): $expanded_path"
   269→}
   270→
